Jim, owner is welcome to one of the early videos in the training, what to expect. I believe expectations are one of the most important things in any relationship, especially when you're starting out, because it can make or break the relationship simply based on what we expect, right? And I think the key to a happy marriage is low expectations, high commitment, and that's kind of the key to a lot of relationships. And so I want to make sure any times that being said, there are people who fail, and I want to outline how and why they fail so that you can avoid those pitfalls. And also so that we can set the proper expectations, because there are many times where people aren't feeling at all, and they're getting five to one, six to one back on their ad spend, and they're expecting 20 to one. And because their expectations were falsely set, they are dissatisfied with something that is making them a ridiculous amount of money at ROI. Believe it or not, it happens quite frequently. And so I want to make sure that we can address this in these videos very candidly so that I can set you up for success and so that you can avoid the common pitfalls that come up, okay? So here are some of the reasons that, these are the top reasons that gyms fail, and then I'll set the expectations on what you can expect from us, what we will expect from you, et cetera, okay? So the first thing is understanding what ROI from having graduated, right? It's kind of a crazy concept if you think about it. Typically you go to school for four years, you acquire a skill, and then you can go to the workforce, right? You pay for the education, and then you get to use the education. Gym launch is powerful enough that we are able to make people enough money fast enough that they're able to find and program that we are trying to give you a specific set of skills that will make you a significantly higher amount of money, all right? Based on the numbers that we, insignificant compared to that, but you have to also understand that sometimes it takes more than a week sometimes, you know what I mean? Sounds kind of crazy. If you were to really, you know what I mean? And so really understanding that the goal is for you to acquire a skill, and that is what we are doing. We are setting the payment structure up so that hopefully you can pay us over time with the skills that you acquire as fast as possible, okay? The next issue that comes up is ROI on ad spend relative versus absolute. And this is a huge one. This is massive, okay? I see this happen so frequently it drives me nuts. And so the average ROI that people in their first six weeks get with us is five and a half to one, okay? That's the actual average. And that's actually before we made some of the big improvements that we have on selling big ticket and things like that. But based on our old numbers, I'll even give you the shittier numbers because I want to set lower expectations for you, $1,000. Gross, right? Then you have your what you'd pay gym lunch, right? And then there's not a lot left over, right? And so the key is to understand, like, is that an amazing ROI? Yes, it's fucking awesome. You know what I mean? It's amazing. But say for example that your ROI drops to three to one, but you spend $5,000, right? It means that you're able to make $15,000, five goes to ads, but you have $10,000 left over, okay? And then there's plenty of room for you to make a profit, okay? And so that was with a small example, right? Now typically it's not going to drop that much, but I just wanted to use it as an extreme example. I have guys, our top gym owners spend $20,000 a month every month. And some of those guys only get like two and a half to one back. But they're okay with that because they spend $20,000 and they make because it's better to get four to one at scale than it is to get 10 to one off of $500. There's as much money to that machine as you can. It's a belief that you have to break, all right? Everyone's afraid to spend money. You have to spend money in order to make money. A lot of times that's the case, okay? Okay? The people who fail change the variables and then expect the same outcome. Like, well, you know, I was thinking about doing it this way and then maybe running my ads like this. Cool. Well, that's not gym lunch, you know what I mean? And so if you want to bear the cost of experimentation then you are welcome to, but do not expect the same outcome, all right? And so when I say don't be cute, like, don't have an ego and think that you are going to be some magical genius. Have the outcome that we're saying, all right? When in reality, all you need to do is do it again, okay? Dan Kennedy said the truth. The gyms who do the best have the discipline to stay boring and consistent and repeat processes that are successful, all right? And you're not going to find silver bullets, all right? There are no silver bullets. Everything is incremental gains, 1% returns that we have done all of this work beforehand before you've gotten on this, before this video was made. I'm going to spend 100, make 300 and have 200 left over rather than the guy who's bragging about how he gets 20 to one on $500. I don't care, right? You're going to ask for lots of money up front. You're going to charge three or four times what you're used to on your services. You're going to provide more value than you ever have before and you're going to help people in a way that you haven't been able to. All right? And those are all going to be new things for you. Next, recommended daily ad spend. You're going to want to spend $100 a day plus between six to 10 ad sets. It's just like... You're not going to know which ones are going to be winners. Now, for those of you paying attention at home, why do you think we would ask you to do $100 a day? How much is the program? We're trying to make you as much money as seemingly possible, as fast as possible so we can pay ourselves off so you can love us forever. All right? So here are the obstacles that usually come up. Sales mindset. You sell out of your own wallet, okay? You're afraid of asking for money. You don't think your things are valuable. You think that taking money from someone is a bad thing, okay? People need to invest. People pay. Like if you can get someone to pay, they will pay attention. How many times have you given your family members free programs? They didn't do anything with it. It's because you didn't take any money. There was no energy exchange, right? You have to get them to exchange something in order to become invested. They have to have skin in the game. The more skin in the game, the better their results. Like I'm telling you this time and time again. All right? When people pay $5,000 for a program that they're trying to lose weight, guess what? They lose more weight than people paying $5, right? You should already know this intuitively, all right? And afraid of charging more. You're literally helping more people. So just break that belief, be done with it. All right? The way to break this, do this every day. So this is one of the things that we had a while back is we had a challenge where we had everyone just listen for 45 minutes before they did their sales. Listen to the sales recordings, all right? And when they did that, everyone's sales went up. Okay? I haven't had anybody who've actually done this listen to 45 minutes a day for like a day. All right? You got to check them. You got to see what ads are working, which ads aren't. Turn them off. Turn off the bad ones. Duplicate the good ones, et cetera. All right? Make sure that you make new ads. All right? Markets get fatigued. All right? Marketing has to be new and different. You have to make it fresh. It has to look and feel different. All right? So make new ads on a regular basis. Make them with your team. Record the warm-ups. Record the workouts. Trainers do it when they're time to do it. If you do it every day during the sessions, you're killing two birds with one stone. And then finally, in terms of reps, watch the videos. We pulled a stat, I think it was a few months ago, from customer service, which was, we had no person who had watched every video in the pool. See you. It's not only watch them all, but watch them all multiple times. Every one of the coaches that we have has gone through the pool three, four, five times. Why? Because every time they go through it, they catch new things, right? Because there's just no way that your human brain, our human brains, can catch something on the first time. It's not. You'll get the general idea, and then you'll catch details. And the devil is in the details. All right? In terms of time commitment, 25 to 30 hours a week is what you should expect to put in. That's just 20 hours right there, right? And on top of that, you're going to want to consume the videos. You have to attend your coaching calls, and you've got to make new ads and monitor them. It's going to be full-time. And if you're like, but I'm running my gym, yes. It requires work ethic. It requires sacrifice. It's a trade-off. But the thing is, is if you're here, what got you to here is not going to get you where you want to go. So this is what the people who make the most money do. All right. One, they attend the daily calls. Attend the daily calls. Ask questions. There's a coach literally there every day. Be available seven days a week, and be available nine to nine, six of those days on Sundays open up three or four hours. Like, you want to make the most money? Be available. That's all I'm trying to do here. I have people in the door make you money. All right. Now, if you're like, you know what? I don't want to do that. I don't want to do the legwork. I kind of like how everything's worked. I just want to keep everything the same. Then you can keep doing it and everything stays exactly the way it is now, which is we basically as soon as whenever you want to jumpstart, you want to do a new thing, you let us know, we'll build it out for you. So hopefully I've set the expectations. I absolutely want you to crush it. You absolutely can crush it. If you were average, you will crush it. What I don't want you to do is be below average. And if you are average, you'll still probably crush it.
